column1: Use 0, 1, or 2 in the Gradebook. Leave only non-applicable cells blank. Applicable counts are automated from the Type and Graph columns. Bonus cells are optional and blank bonus cells count as 0 bonus.

column5: 0 = Not met
column6: 1 = Partially met
column7: 2 = Fully met
column8: Announcement basis

column5: Problem or research question is vague, too broad, or not testable/measurable.
column6: A problem/question exists, but scope, target, or success criterion is still fuzzy.
column7: Problem/question is specific, scoped, and measurable; inputs, outputs, and objective are clear.
column8: General ML requirement: clear problem formulation. Evaluation criterion 1: realism, clarity, relevance.

column5: No clear user, decision being automated/augmented, deployer, or payer.
column6: Some stakeholders are mentioned, but deployment or use context is incomplete.
column7: Clearly identifies the user, the decision being automated/augmented, and a realistic deployer or payer.
column8: Option A mandatory requirements: define decision, who would deploy/pay.

column5: Research question or target venue/benchmark is missing.
column6: Both are mentioned, but the match is weak, vague, or under-justified.
column7: Clear research question and an appropriate target venue/benchmark are explicitly stated.
column8: Option B mandatory conditions: research question and target venue.

column4: A: why ML, why not a simpler non-AI solution
column5: No real justification for ML, or the work could obviously be handled by a simpler non-AI approach.
column6: Some justification exists, but it relies on generic claims or weak reasoning.
column7: Gives a concrete reason a simpler non-AI solution is insufficient and why ML is appropriate beyond buzzwords.
column8: Option A mandatory requirements: why a non-AI solution is insufficient; why ML is appropriate.

column4: B: why research, not application + publishable scope
column5: Could easily be framed as an application project, or the final scope is not plausibly publishable.
column6: Some research motivation exists, but the case for non-application framing or publishability is uncertain.
column7: Strong case that the work is genuinely research-driven and that the scope/output is plausibly publishable.
column8: Option B mandatory conditions and hard rule: not application-framed; plausibly publishable.

column5: Importance is asserted only, with no credible evidence or reasoning.
column6: Relevance is described, but evidence, constraints, or stakes are still weak.
column7: Importance, constraints, and likely impact/significance are supported with evidence or credible domain reasoning.
column8: Option A: real-world problem and market/pain point. Option B: inherently research-driven and significant.

column4: Project type/track fit and success criteria
column5: Chosen type/track does not fit the work, or success criteria are absent.
column6: Fit is mostly reasonable, but success criteria or alignment are incomplete.
column7: Chosen type/track is appropriate and success criteria are explicit and aligned.
column8: Track alignment from prior rubric + announcement requirement to choose exactly one type.

column4: Task formulation, target, and data visibility
column5: Inputs, outputs, task type, or data access/visibility are unclear or incorrect.
column6: Most pieces are present, but key assumptions or data details are under-specified.
column7: Task, target, data availability, and assumptions are correctly defined.
column8: General ML requirements: clear problem formulation, proper data visibility.

column5: No valid non-AI baseline, or comparison is not on the same task/data.
column6: A baseline exists, but it is weak, unfair, or incomplete.
column7: A clear non-AI baseline is implemented on the same task/data with a fair comparison.
column8: Option A technical requirements: at least one explicit non-AI baseline.

column5: No meaningful baseline or prior-work comparison is provided.
column6: A comparison exists, but it is weak, incomplete, or not quite fair.
column7: Strong, fair comparison to appropriate baseline(s) or prior work on the same task/data.
column8: General ML requirements: baseline comparison. Research expectation: competitive experimental grounding.

column4: ML method choice and substantive contribution
column5: Method is inappropriate, unexplained, or just a thin wrapper around a pretrained model with no substantive ML work.
column6: Method is somewhat justified, but choices or trade-offs are weakly defended.
column7: Method choice is appropriate, clearly justified, and shows substantive ML design beyond simple wrapping.
column8: Technical requirements + hard rule against just wrapping a pretrained model.

column5: Major leakage or preprocessing errors are present.
column6: Pipeline mostly works, but some steps or justifications are weak.
column7: Preprocessing/features are correct, leakage-aware, and technically sound.
column8: Prior rubric: preprocessing, feature engineering. General ML soundness.

column5: Splits, metrics, or evaluation protocol are missing or seriously flawed.
column6: Evaluation exists, but the design or metric choice is only partly convincing.
column7: Train/validation/test (or equivalent), justified metrics, and a reproducible evaluation protocol are sound.
column8: Sound experimentation: splits, metrics, justified evaluation metrics.

column5: No real error analysis or failure cases are shown.
column6: Some errors/examples are shown, but the analysis is shallow.
column7: Concrete failure modes are analyzed and connected to model behavior.
column8: Option A technical requirements: error analysis and failure cases. Critical thinking criterion.

column5: Limitations are ignored, hidden, or misrepresented.
column6: Some limitations are mentioned, but discussion is superficial.
column7: Limitations and trade-offs are discussed honestly and with technical accuracy.
column8: General ML requirements: honest discussion of limitations.

column5: No plausible research artifact beyond 'a model ran'.
column6: Some artifact exists (draft, analysis, ablation, dataset work), but it is shallow or incomplete.
column7: Strong research artifact such as a paper draft, novel analysis, benchmark/dataset, or meaningful ablation package.
column8: Option B expected outputs: paper draft, new benchmark/dataset, or novel experimental analysis.

column4: Graph is a core modeling object
column5: Graph is only used for visualization/storage, or nodes/edges/relations are not clearly defined.
column6: Graph has some modeling role, but representation is incomplete or weakly justified.
column7: Graph is a core modeling object with clear nodes, edges, and relations.
column8: Additional requirement for graph-based projects: graph must be core object; define nodes/edges/relations.

column5: No non-graph baseline or no justification for the graph structure.
column6: One part exists, but the baseline or justification is weak/incomplete.
column7: Fair non-graph baseline on the same raw data and a clear explanation of what graph structure adds.
column8: Graph requirements: compare against non-graph baseline; justify why graph captures added information.

column5: Not addressed.
column6: Mentioned or lightly demonstrated, but without real insight.
column7: Meaningfully addressed with an interpretation or explainability method tied to decisions/errors.
column8: Responsible ML requirement: explainability or interpretability.

column5: Not addressed.
column6: Bias/fairness risks are mentioned, but no concrete check is shown.
column7: A concrete fairness concern is identified and evaluated or mitigated.
column8: Responsible ML requirement: bias or fairness concerns.

column5: Not addressed.
column6: Risk is acknowledged, but no concrete handling is shown.
column7: A concrete privacy or leakage risk is identified and addressed with checks or design decisions.
column8: Responsible ML requirement: privacy or data leakage risks.

column5: Not addressed.
column6: Robustness or distribution shift is mentioned, but with little evidence.
column7: Concrete robustness or distribution-shift analysis or mitigation is shown.
column8: Responsible ML requirement: robustness or failure under distribution shift.

column5: No working Dockerized API.
column6: Partial API or Docker setup exists, but it is not reliably runnable end-to-end.
column7: Working Dockerized API with a clear run path.
column8: Option A production requirement + final deliverable: running API.

column4: Separation of data processing, model logic, and serving
column5: Code is monolithic or the boundaries are tangled.
column6: Some separation exists, but boundaries are unclear.
column7: Clear separation between data processing, model logic, and serving.
column8: Option A production requirement: clear separation between data processing, model logic, and serving.

column5: Project cannot be reliably reproduced or rerun.
column6: Some instructions/env files exist, but reproduction is incomplete or fragile.
column7: Environment, versions, seeds/checkpoints, and instructions make reruns straightforward.
column8: Production requirement: reproducible experiments.

column4: Functional UI or demo flow appropriate to project type
column5: Missing or nonfunctional demo/UI.
column6: Minimal demo works, but it is incomplete, brittle, or poorly aligned to the project type.
column7: Functional end-to-end demo/UI appropriate to the project type. For Option B, a minimal but effective demo endpoint/runner is acceptable.
column8: Option A production requirement: minimal but functional user interface.

column4: Running deployed artifact or stable service
column5: No deployed artifact/service and no clear evaluation run path.
column6: Runs locally or is partially deployed, but not reliably presented.
column7: Running deployed artifact/API or a very clear, stable service setup suitable for evaluation.
column8: Final deliverables: running API (cloud of your choice).

column5: Repository structure is confusing or inconsistent.
column6: Mostly organized, but some folders/files are unclear.
column7: Clear folder structure and naming throughout the repository.
column8: Final deliverables: well-structured GitHub repository.

column5: README is missing or insufficient for setup/run.
column6: README exists, but key setup, run, or usage details are missing.
column7: README clearly covers setup, run, usage, and quick start.
column8: Prior rubric: README with installation steps and usage examples.

column5: System or method is unclear from the repository.
column6: Some documentation exists, but the architecture/method is not fully understandable.
column7: Documentation makes the system/method understandable without digging through code.
column8: Prior rubric: system architecture and documentation.

column4: Results tables, logs, experiment notes, ablations
column5: No real experiment record or result trace is documented.
column6: Some results are shown, but logs/tables/ablations are incomplete.
column7: Baseline comparisons, results tables, logs, or ablations are clearly documented.
column8: Prior rubric: experiment notes, comparison tables, logs.

column4: Data sources, limitations, ethics/compliance, deployment notes
column5: Important context such as data source, limitations, or deployment notes is missing.
column6: Some notes exist, but important context is still missing.
column7: Data sources, limitations, ethics/compliance, and deployment notes are documented.
column8: General ML limitation discussion + prior rubric deployment notes.

column5: Audience cannot tell what the project is solving or why it matters.
column6: Problem is stated, but value/motivation is not crisp.
column7: Problem and motivation are immediately clear.
column8: Poster/final presentation expectations: problem definition.

column5: Pipeline explanation is confusing or incorrect.
column6: Mostly understandable, but key steps are missing or rushed.
column7: Method/architecture explanation is logical and technically clear.
column8: Presentation expectations: architecture, ML approach, deployment plan.

column5: Demo/results are unclear, unreadable, or misleading.
column6: Some useful visuals/results are shown, but not fully clear.
column7: Results/demo visuals are clear, relevant, and honest about performance.
column8: Presentation expectations: evaluation, UI, results.

column5: Team cannot answer basic technical questions.
column6: Team answers some questions, but shows important gaps or avoidance.
column7: Team answers questions directly and shows strong project ownership.
column8: Final presentation: 5 minutes + Q&A.

column4: Originality of idea or research angle
column5: Very derivative, with no meaningful original angle.
column6: Some novelty in framing or combination exists, but originality is limited.
column7: Clear original angle in idea, framing, or research question.
column8: Prior rubric: novelty / innovation.

column5: Decisions appear arbitrary or unexamined.
column6: Some trade-offs are mentioned, but not thoughtfully justified.
column7: Design choices show thoughtful trade-off reasoning.
column8: Prior rubric: thoughtful design decisions.

column5: Only the minimum requirements are met.
column6: Some extra effort exists, but impact is limited.
column7: Meaningful effort beyond minimum requirements with clear value.
column8: Prior rubric: effort beyond the basic requirements.

column4: Purposeful extra technical features or polish
column5: Extras are absent or gimmicky.
column6: Extras exist, but are weakly integrated.
column7: Extra technical features or polish are purposeful and well integrated.
column8: Prior rubric: extra technical features, polished design.

column5: Not present, simulated without constraints, or unjustified.
column6: Some real edge/mobile work exists, but constraints or justification are weak.
column7: Real edge/mobile deployment or on-device inference under meaningful constraints is justified.
column8: Announcement bonus: edge/mobile deployment gets positive consideration.

column5: Only meets the minimum Responsible ML requirement or duplicates base-rubric work.
column6: Goes somewhat beyond minimum with at least one extra check, tool, or experiment.
column7: Materially exceeds minimum with strong additional analysis/tooling across responsible ML dimensions.
column8: Optional bonus aligned with responsible ML depth; do not double count minimum required work.

column4: Exceptional extension beyond the base rubric
column5: No exceptional extension beyond the base rubric.
column6: One standout extension exists, but depth is moderate.
column7: Exceptional extension such as strong ablations, benchmark release, advanced tooling, or unusual production polish.
column8: Prior bonus idea: extra-mile work beyond base requirements.
